observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 

1.	Draw the necessary journal entries to record the valid book adjustments and reconciling items to achieve a fair presentation of the cash in bank account;

2.	Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a thorough review of the prior years’ unidentified reconciling items for the proper adjustments in the books and arrive at a correct cash-in-bank balance; and

3.	Regularly prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements of all funds for timely update of the cash in bank account on any reconciling items and adjustments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

4.	The Property and Accounting Units reconcile their respective records, taking into consideration the items not recorded in the accounting books but found during the inventory taking, and items recorded in the PPE books but were not found or non-existent during the physical count; and

5.	The Municipal Accountant drew the necessary adjusting entries to establish the correct and accurate year-end account balance of the properties in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Property and Accounting records are still not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: 10.	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly supply the required amounts and details of the unexpended LDRRMF balances in the notes to financial statements to expound the understanding of the financial statement users and decision-makers.
observations.reason: Reconciliation was made after submission of year-end FS-2024.

observations.recommendation:
13.Direct the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to i) prepare and update PPELCs and Property Cards, respectively; and ii) reconcile both records; and

14.	require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for the listed PPE since they were ready for use, and moving forward, always provide depreciation of assets.
observations.reason: Not all PPE were provided their corresponding depreciation.

observations.recommendation:
21.	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects shall be considered in the recapitulation of the Development Projects in the Annual Budget to afford the public with the benefits that can be derived thereat.

22.	Also, enjoin the MPDO and Engineering Unit on the proper coordination during planning period to ascertain that only those viably implementable projects be considered in the list to be funded out from the Development Fund.
observations.reason:
This is reiterated in CY 2024




This is reiterated in CY 2024

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Mayor require the Accountant to:

24.	prepare the BRS for all bank accounts maintained by the Municipality and promptly submit the same to the Audit Team; and

25.	conduct a thorough review of the Cash-Local Treasury account, so that the unaccounted balance could be traced/identified, and effect necessary adjustments after verification, duly supported with documents.

26.	We further recommended that the Mayor direct the Accountant and the Treasurer to reconcile their records, and henceforth, conduct regular reconciliation of the balances between the accounting records and the treasury cashbooks, to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the reported balances of the Cash accounts.
observations.reason: The records between the treasurer and accounting are still not reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Draw the necessary journal entry vouchers (JEVs) to record the valid book reconciling items;
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a thorough review of the unidentified reconciling items reflected in the BRS for its proper adjustments and support the same with relevant documentary evidence to ascertain the fair presentation of the financial statements; and
Prepare monthly BRS before the financial statements for timely update of cash in bank account, and submit the same with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that:
The City Accountant draws a journal entry to record the motor vehicle received from the DOH;
The Supply Officer issues the PAR of the ambulance, including the equipment and accessories to the end-user; and
Henceforth, the Management provides the Supply Officer and Municipal Account copies of transfer and receipt invoices such as Property Transfer Report and Deed of Donations for subsequent donations that the Municipality may receive.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended CY 2024 and 2023 LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide records to support the ₱7,975,229.84 balance and make necessary adjusting entries, if any, to update the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account. And henceforth, maintain SLs for STF by year of transfer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly complies with the pertinent provisions of Section 127 of IPSAS 1, Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016, and Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, regarding the disclosure of relevant information in the Notes to Financial Statements, particularly for Cash in Bank – LCCA, Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities, and the amounts and details of the unexpended LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Planning Development Officer and the Budget Officer to ensure that information such as specific timelines and expected outputs are defined in the AIP to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the PPAs, and
Ensure that only those well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready development projects shall be funded out of the 20% DF to afford the constituents with the benefits that can be derived thereof.
We recommended that the Municipality execute an Individual Job Order Contract when they engage the services of a Job Order Personnel.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the LDRRM Council, strictly consider the pre-requisite conditions before an original appropriation is subjected to reprogramming or reversion to ascertain the propriety and validity of the expenditures.
We recommended that the GFPS of the Municipality submits the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report within the period prescribed by regulation.
We recommended that the designated GAD Focal Person of the City to prepare the GAD Agenda to ensure proper monitoring and evaluation of GAD results and outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office spearheads the setting up and maintenance of the GAD Database by coordinating with the DILG Field Operation’s Officer and other concerned LGU officials. Further, we recommend the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data, which will provide the basis for gender analysis, planning, programming, and policy formulation.

We recommended the Accountant reconcile the prior years’ unidentified balance of the Due to GSIS account, remit the unremitted withheld amounts to GSIS, and henceforth ensure that the remittance of the withheld amounts is within the prescribed period.

6.	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately prepare the necessary corrections and adjust entries to attain the correct balance of the accumulated depreciation and achieve a fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:

7.	Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to derecognize the fully liquidated fund transfer from NGAs; and

8.	Strictly comply with the proper timing of derecognition of the Due to NGAs account after the eventual submission of liquidation and settlement of the amount to ascertain a fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

9.	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant supply the required disclosures in the notes to financial statements on the reported accounts to expound the user’s understanding of the financial statements.

11.	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the MDC-EC, strictly consider the pre-requisite conditions before an original appropriation is subjected to reprogramming or reversion to ascertain the propriety and validity of the changes made to the Annual Budget.

We recommended that the:

15.	Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of each year, a duly certified list of tax payers and the amount due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (PRTAR) or Tax payer’s Index Card; and

16.	Municipal Accountant set-up the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer, in compliance with the modified accrual method of accounting for income from real property taxes, in accordance with the NGAS Manual Volume I for LGUs.

17.	We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to fairly present the current and non-current portion of its Loans Payable account and to provide sufficient information in the Notes to give additional value to the financial statements of the LGU for relevant decision-making of its users and other readers.

18.	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the required monthly bank reconciliation statements for all the funds and bank accounts of the LGU to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the reported balances.

19.	Also, enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to come up with strategy for the timely completion of their financial reports for submission to the Accounting Unit to sustain the required timely preparation of BRS and correction of the Cash account.
20.We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit regularly to the Bureau of Treasury, the national agency’s shares from collection.

23.	We recommended that the LDRRMO, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their respective records to arrive at the accurate unutilized amount which should have been transferred to the Special Trust Fund. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare separate RAAOs for the QRF, MOOE, and CO of the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Mayor require the Accountant to:

27.	stop granting additional advances to AOs until previous cash advances are fully settled/liquidated in the books.

28.	Require all the Revenue Collecting Officers to submit daily the Report of Collections and Deposit to the Municipal Treasurer for her to prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms on a regular basis; and

29.	Submit the same to the Audit Team not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month duly supported with the individual Reports of Accountability for Accountable forms of each accountable officer.
30. The Municipality should adhere to the mandates of R.A. 658 for it to avail of financial considerations inherent in insurances should any calamity occur.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Draw the necessary journal entries to record the valid book adjustments
and reconciling items to achieve a fair presentation of the cash in bank
account;
b) Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a thorough review of
the prior years’ unidentified reconciling items for the proper adjustments
in the books and arrive at a correct cash-in-bank balance; and
c) Regularly prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements
of all funds for timely update of the cash in bank account on any
reconciling items and adjustments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The Property and Accounting Units reconcile their respective records,
taking into consideration the items not recorded in the accounting books
but found during the inventory taking, and items recorded in the PPE
books but were not found or non-existent during the physical count; and
b. The Municipal Accountant drew the necessary adjusting entries to
establish the correct and accurate year-end account balance of the
properties in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately prepare the
necessary corrections and adjust entries to attain the correct balance of the
accumulated depreciation and achieve a fair presentation of the account in the
financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to derecognize the fully
liquidated fund transfer from NGAs; and
b. Strictly comply with the proper timing of derecognition of the Due to NGAs
account after the eventual submission of liquidation and settlement of the
amount to ascertain a fair presentation of the account in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant supply the required
disclosures in the notes to financial statements on the reported accounts to
expound the user’s understanding of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly supply the required
amounts and details of the unexpended LDRRMF balances in the notes to
financial statements to expound the understanding of the financial statement
users and decision-makers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the MDC-
EC, strictly consider the pre-requisite conditions before an original
appropriation is subjected to reprogramming or reversion to ascertain the
propriety and validity of the changes made to the Annual Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRMO include
in the LDRRMFIP the prior year balances under the STF within the five-year
validity period during budget preparations and deliberations to augment the
support to disaster risk reduction and management activities beneficial to the
community.

1.14 We recommended that the Mayor require the Accountant to:
a. prepare the BRS for all bank accounts maintained by the Municipality
and promptly submit the same to the Audit Team; and
b. conduct a thorough review of the Cash-Local Treasury account, so that the
unaccounted balance could be traced/identified, and effect necessary
adjustments after verification, duly supported with documents.
1.15 We further recommended that the Mayor direct the Accountant and the
Treasurer to reconcile their records, and henceforth, conduct regular
reconciliation of the balances between the accounting records and the
treasury cashbooks, to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the reported
balances of the Cash accounts.

2.14 We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Accountant to:
a. determine the causes of the negative balances in the Due to National
Government Agencies and Due to LGUs accounts, and effect adjusting
entries as necessary;
b. reclassify the funds that were not sourced from the LGUs to their
proper accounts;
c. conduct a thorough review and analysis of the liability accounts, and
draw adjustments to accurately present the balances of the accounts in
the financial statements; and
d. stop the practice of recording the receipts from PhilHealth and the
proceeds from sale transactions as liability instead of recognizing
income, and henceforth, record the proper accounting treatment of the
transactions in the books.

3.9 We recommended that the Mayor require the Accountant to:
a. draw the following adjusting entries in the books for the erroneous
recording of fund transfers in CY 2021:
Debit Credit
Due from Local Government Units 1,440,000.00
Prior Period Adjustments 1,440,000.00
b. stop the practice of recording as expense the fund transfers, and
henceforth, recognize the receivable account Due from Local
Government Units account for every transfer of fund to Barangays
intended for specific purposes.
3.10 We further recommended that the Municipality include in the MOA of fund
transfers the specific period of liquidation, in order to monitor the utilization
and implementation of the PPAs and enforce prompt submission of
liquidation reports by the implementing barangays.

4.14 We recommended that the Mayor require the Accountant to:
a. send out demand letters/notices to the AOs, enforcing the immediate
liquidation of their outstanding cash advances;
b. conduct periodic monitoring of the submission of liquidation reports to
facilitate timely accounting thereof and prevent undue accumulation of
outstanding cash advances;
c. stop granting additional advances to AOs until previous cash advances
are fully settled /liquidated in the books; and
d. ascertain the cause of the negative balances in the Advances for
Operating Expenses and Advances for Payroll accounts, and make
necessary adjustments in the books after verification.

5.17 We recommended that the Mayor:
1. instruct the Accountant to –
a. transfer the unexpended balances of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and
the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund at year end;
b. recognize in the books all existing inventories of DRRM, and henceforth,
coordinate with the personnel in-charge/designated Supply Officer for the
list of undistributed supplies, materials or relief goods at year end;
c. include in the Notes to Financial Statements the disclosure requirements
on DRRM prescribed under COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.
2. require the designated Supply/Property Officer for DRRM to –
a. comply with the preparation and submission of the Inventory List of Items
for DRRM, duly supported with Annexes P-1 and P-2, as required under
COA Circular No. 2014-002; and
b. conduct periodic reconciliation of property records with the accounting
records and furnish the Accounting Office of pertinent reports relating to
procured DRRM items, their distribution and balances at year end, in
order that the Accountant could record the same in the books.

6.4 We recommended that all funds received by the Municipality intended
for COVID-19 related programs/activities/expenses be timely utilized for
their intended purposes, to ensure that the intended beneficiaries
immediately benefit therefrom.

7.8 We recommended that the Mayor direct the Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the other departments and focal persons of the various
SAs to gather available documents for unsupported dormant fund
balances;
b. prepare and submit liquidation reports immediately after verification
and return the unused balances of completed PPAs to the SAs;
c. immediately cause the return of the unexpended PDAF balances to the
Bureau of Treasury; and
d. henceforth, maintain subsidiary records, retain copies of MOA and
such other documents for every fund received for the undertaking of
specific purpose.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant:
to rectify errors in recording that may have caused the irregular debit/negative balance;  
determine the source and purpose of all the long-outstanding balances in the General Fund books of the Due to NGAs account and stop the practice of recording the transfers to this account; and 
to cause the return of balances of various completed programs/projects to the Source Agencies as provided for in COA Circular 94-013.
observations.reason: Our recommendations were not given much attention.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to record/transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Special Trust Fund to present a reliable balance of the account. Further, we also recommend to properly disclose the details of the account in the Notes to Financial Statements in order to further facilitate the preparation of LDRRMFIP for disaster related management activities within the next five (5) years.
observations.reason: Our recommendations were not given much attention.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the MDRRM Officials to strictly follow the above rules and regulation in preparing the LDRRMFIP in order to utilize the LDRRMF for disaster management related activities that would be useful in the locality.
observations.reason: Our recommendation was not given much attention

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC submit APP-CSE either printed copy or electronic copy through online to PS-DBM for the succeeding years in order to include the needs of the Municipality of the commonly-use supplies in the projection of inventory requirements, scheduling of procurement activities, and overall management in order to subsequently purchase all CSE in the PS-DBM depot.
observations.reason: The BAC did not submit APP-CSE to PS-DBM during CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the RCCs remit or turn-over their collections within the specified period stated above.  Failure to do so will subject the accountable officer to disciplinary actions such as suspension in handling money accountability and accountable forms as well as being detailed to another office.
observations.reason: RCCs were not compelled to remit their collections daily.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned municipal officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Our recommendation was not given much attention.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to trace back the erroneous transactions and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in order to present fairly the financial statements.
Moreover, we also recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain the required subsidiary ledgers for every account
observations.reason: There is still negative balance in asset account, particularly the Advances for payroll account amounting to ₱195,312.40.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall institute legal actions against the contractor for failure to complete the project within the period specified in the contract, including but not limited to:

Direct LGU officials concerned or the project engineer to explain the inaction or lapses in their responsibilities which caused the delay or maybe non-completion of the project. 
Issue demand letter to the contractor by requiring them to submit a catch-up plan and compel them to complete the project as soon as possible.  
Impose Liquidated Damages
Terminate the contract, if warranted.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer/BUB Focal person to strictly monitor the implementation of BUB projects to avoid the recurrence of the noted deficiency for the general welfare of the end users of the project.
observations.reason: No liquidated damages were imposed during the final billing by the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management must properly prepare and review the projects and activities for the LDRRMF in accordance with the NDDRMC, DBM and DILG JMC 2013-1.  Further, the fund must be fully and appropriately utilized for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery.
observations.reason: There were still expenditures which were not disaster related, particularly the disbursement for catering services during the conduct of Conduct of MDRRM Council Regular & Emergency Meetings.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board immediately update/revise and submit the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to DENR-EMB for approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission as required under Section 16 of RA 9003 for the purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.

Moreover, the Board shall make representation with the SB Council for the inclusion in the annual budget Solid Waste Management Programs, Projects and Activities for immediate and proper implementation.
observations.reason: The LGU did not submit to the audit team a revised MSW Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Inventory Committee, if any to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all properties of LGU.
Direct the GSO to submit the complete Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards (PPELC), respectively and conduct a periodic reconciliation of records in accordance with Section 114 and 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I.
observations.reason:
The GSO did not submit the complete RPCPPE to the Audit Team.

The Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant have yet to maintain complete Property and Ledger Cards for each PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the unreconciled items and record the valid reconciling items in the general ledger pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 so that the accuracy and reliability of Cash in Bank balances could be ascertained.


We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should regularly prepare and submit bank reconciliation for its cash in bank account and adjust the books accordingly, pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant cannot determine the nature of the unreconciled items.


The Municipal Accountant did not regularly submit bank reconciliation statement to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts and ensure correct computation based on the guidelines set in PPSAS 17. Then, prepare all the necessary journal entries to correct the account balances.
observations.reason: The GSO and the MA are still reconciling the balances of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts, financial statements and reports and accompanying schedules within the period prescribed by existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The Management implies that it is due to lack of workforce in the accounting office.

Prior Years’ unexpended balances were not embodied in LDRRMFIP and budget.


The Utilization Report was not regularly submitted to the Audit Team.


No transfer of unexpended LDRRMF balance to STF during the year.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the use of the imprest fund system in maintaining the LGU’s Petty Cash Fund and ensure that the same is liquidated at year end in consonance with the provisions of Section 48, NGAS Manual for LGU, Vol. I.
observations.reason: The accounting office has still to reconcile with the PCF custodian with regards to the unreplenished balance of PCF in the FS.

observations.recommendation: Require all the Revenue Collecting Officers to submit daily the Report of Collections and Deposit to the Municipal Treasurer for her to prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms on a regular basis and to submit the same to the Audit Team not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month duly supported with the individual Reports of Accountability for Accountable forms of each accountable officer.
observations.reason:
RCCs did not submit their RCD to the Municipal Treasurer daily. 


The Accountable Officer prepared the CRAAF only during the conduct of cash examination.

observations.recommendation: The municipality should adhere to the mandates of R.A. 658 for it to avail of financial considerations inherent in insurances should any calamity occur.
observations.reason: GSIS personnel has yet to return for the evaluation of LGU’s properties and assessment thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instructs the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete supply ledger cards and strictly adhere to the regulations on the recording of receipt and issuance of inventory items, as well as controlling and monitoring of the same. Also, to prepare adjusting entries for the noted deficiencies; and
directs the Supply Officer to maintain complete stock cards, regularly prepare the RIS and RSMI, conduct the annual physical count of all inventories on stock as well as those transferred to distributing site to determine any balances on hand, and to prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to complete the depreciation for all depreciable PPEs, establish the accumulated depreciation accounts as well as record adjustments in the Government Equity account for prior years’ unrecorded depreciation.  The Audit Team further recommends that the Municipal Accountant prepare Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation, to be updated regularly, to serve as guide in the computation of periodic provision of depreciation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to record payments of progress billing in CIP accounts and refrain from immediately recording said payments to PPE accounts.  Likewise, mandate the assigned personnel to expedite processing of the required document/s necessary for the financing to facilitate full payment of the project, in order to subsequently inflict corresponding depreciation on the completed project.

We recommended that the LCE direct the accountant to make the necessary adjustments to the affected accounts according to Paragraph 44 and 45 of IPSAS 23 and to make appropriate disclosure in the Notes to FS regarding the utilization of the BGCM Fund to provide a more accurate and reliable financial statement.
We recommended that management comply with the provision in Section 3.7 of LBC 125 for the creation of SAGF for the BGCM.

We recommended that the management immediately submit the lacking documents and ensure that henceforth, the required documents for emergency procurements (negotiated) are complete before payments are made in accordance with the provisions of the Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set by GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 to prevent the possibility of abuses and corruption in the undertaking of its emergency procurements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDC and MPD Office to prepare an approved Supplemental or Amended AIP for newly identified PPAs that will be sourced out of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Management:
strictly comply the regulations on the grant of the COVID-19 Hazard Pay and make payment only to those qualified personnel; and
avoid the charging of COVID-19 Hazard Pay and other related benefits to the Trust Fund - DRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to refrain from charging administrative and non-development related expenses against the 20% Development Fund and strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat, BAC and/or the Municipal Engineering Office to furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 for its prompt review and evaluation of the transactions and communication of deficiencies, if any. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require that all contracts involving infrastructure projects be notarized to properly safeguard the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 05 AAR – 2018
observations.reason: Implemented |

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management must properly prepare and review the projects and activities for the LDRRMF in accordance with the NDDRMC, DBM and DILG JMC 2013-1.  Further, the fund must be fully and appropriately utilized for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 06 AAR – 2018
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | LDRRMF for the next succeeding year were already utilized for non-disaster related expenditures as appropriated in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board immediately update/revise and submit the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to DENR-EMB for approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission as required under Section 16 of RA 9003 for the purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.

Moreover, the Board shall make representation with the SB Council for the inclusion in the annual budget Solid Waste Management Programs, Projects and Activities for immediate and proper implementation.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 07 AAR – 2018
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | The LGU didn’t submit to the audit team a revised MSW Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Inventory Committee, if any to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all properties of LGU.
Direct the GSO to submit the complete Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards (PPELC), respectively and conduct a periodic reconciliation of records in accordance with Section 114 and 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 01 AAR - 2017
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | The Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant have yet to maintain complete Property and Ledger Cards for each PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the unreconciled items and record the valid reconciling items in the general ledger pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 so that the accuracy and reliability of Cash in Bank balances could be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 04 AAR – 2017
observations.reason: Not Implemented | The Municipal Accountant cannot determine the nature of the unreconciled items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Revenue Collection Clerks to turn over their collections to the Liquidating Officer/Treasurer in accordance with Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-383 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 09 AAR - 2017
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | The RCCs were still delayed in the turnover of their collections. This observation was reiterated in this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

Constitute an inventory committee to conduct a physical inventory of all properties of LGU.

Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards (PPELC), respectively and conduct a periodic reconciliation of records in accordance with Section 114 and 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 01 AAR-2016
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | The Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant have yet to maintain complete Property and Ledger Cards for each PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Revenue Collection Clerks, through the Municipal Treasurer, to turn over daily their collections to avoid misuse/misappropriation. Also, direct the treasurer to verify the Statement of Collections and Accountable Forms to ensure that actual collections are reported and remitted.

observations.reason: Partially Implemented | The RCCs were still delayed in the turnover of their collections. This observation was reiterated in this Report.

observations.managementAction: Finding No. 06 AAR-2016

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts and ensure correct computation based on the guidelines set in PPSAS 17. Then, prepare all the necessary journal entries to correct the account balances.
observations.managementAction: No Action Taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The GSO and the MA are still reconciling the balances of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned municipal officials to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the proper accounting and reporting for local road and road network system to ensure that all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial statements.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to trace back the erroneous transactions and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in order to present fairly the financial statements.

Moreover, we also recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain the required subsidiary ledgers for every account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile her Loans Payable record with that of the lending institutions so as to present fairly the financial statements.  Moreover, to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall institute legal actions against the contractor for failure to complete the project within the period specified in the contract, including but not limited to:
Direct LGU officials concerned or the project engineer to explain the inaction or lapses in their responsibilities which caused the delay or maybe non-completion of the project.
Issue demand letter to the contractor by requiring them to submit a catch-up plan and compel them to complete the project as soon as possible.
Impose Liquidated Damages
Terminate the contract, if warranted.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer/BUB Focal person to strictly monitor the implementation of BUB projects to avoid the recurrence of the noted deficiency for the general welfare of the end users of the project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat, BAC and/or the Municipal Engineering Office to furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 for its prompt review and evaluation of the transactions and communication of deficiencies, if any.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require that all contracts involving infrastructure projects be notarized to properly safeguard the interest of the government.

We recommended that management must properly prepare and review the projects and activities for the LDRRMF in accordance with the NDDRMC, DBM and DILG JMC 2013-1.  Further, the fund must be fully and appropriately utilized for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board immediately update/revise and submit the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to DENR-EMB for approval by the National Solid Waste Management Commission as required under Section 16 of RA 9003 for the purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management.

Moreover, the Board shall make representation with the SB Council for the inclusion in the annual budget Solid Waste Management Programs, Projects and Activities for immediate and proper implementation.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Inventory Committee, if any to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all properties of LGU.
Direct the GSO to submit the complete Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards (PPELC), respectively and conduct a periodic reconciliation of records in accordance with Section 114 and 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I
observations.managementAction: Management conducted complete inventory of PPE.
observations.reason: The Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant have yet to maintain complete Property and Ledger Cards for each PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting journal entries and observe proper recording of transactions to provide fair financial statement presentation.

We recommend that the local chief executive direct the officials concerned to strictly comply with the provisions stated in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to assure that the government attains the most advantageous price on the procurement of goods and services.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the unreconciled items and record the valid reconciling items in the general ledger pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 so that the accuracy and reliability of Cash in Bank balances could be ascertained.

We recommend that the local chief executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to limit the appropriations for personal services to 55% of the total annual income from regular sources of the next preceding fiscal year so as not to prejudice the priority needs of the municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel concerned to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004, so that the public will be properly informed of the projects undertaken by the agency to promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the formulation of action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team, copy furnished the DBM, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance as mandated by Section 88 of RA 10924.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Revenue Collection Clerks to turn over their collections to the Liquidating Officer/Treasurer in accordance with Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-383 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer issued a Memo to RCCs for the implementation of the recommendation.
observations.reason: The RCCs were still delayed in the turnover of their collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to observe the deadline set in Section 9 of RA 5447 on the remittance of provincial share accruing to the SEF so that the Provincial Government can timely utilize the collections to finance various projects, programs, and activities beneficial to the constituents of the Province of Eastern Samar.

Constitute an inventory committee to conduct a physical inventory of all properties of LGU.
Require the GSO to prepare the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the Audit Team; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards (PPELC), respectively and conduct a periodic reconciliation of records in accordance with Section 114 and 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I.
observations.reason: Management is still conducting an inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Executive Chief direct the concerned officials to include only programs/projects/activities in the AIP which directly support the Philippine Development Plan, Medium Term Public Investment Plan and the Annual Investment Program and adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memo No, 2011-001. And the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to refrain from charging administrative and non-development related expenses against the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Our recommendation was not given much attention.
Prior years’ balance for the unexpended balance for MOOE is still to be determined by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: Require all the Revenue Collecting Officers to submit daily the Report of Collections and Deposit to the Municipal Treasurer for her to prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms on a regular basis and to submit the same to the Audit Team not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month duly supported with the individual Reports of Accountability for Accountable forms of each accountable officer.
observations.reason: Our recommendation was not given much attention.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the documentary requirements for payments of hauling contracts in compliance with Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Henceforth, ensure that subsequent hauling services are duly supported with complete documentation.
observations.reason: Not aware of the additional documentary requirements

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly adhere with the afore-cited laws and regulations.  In addition, the Honorable Mayor should direct the concerned LGU officials and employees to comply and submit the above-listed reports with complete documentations to the COA Audit Team to enable them to come up with audit results which may support significant management decisions.
The purchase of Drugs and Medicines totaling ₱636,956.85 were out rightly recorded under the expense account contrary to Section 51, Volume 1 of the COA Circular 2002-003.
observations.managementAction: Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the supplies as assets under the Inventory account and use the Requisition Issue Slips upon the issuance of these medicines in accordance with Circular 2002-003.
observations.reason: Our recommendation was not given much attention hence purchase of medicines were still recorded as an outright expense

observations.recommendation: Strictly follow the provisions set-forth under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. Ensure that programs/projects/activities prioritized under the Annual Investment Plan are appropriately implemented according to their intended purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management still continues to implement the said recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
Constitute an inventory committee to conduct a physical inventory of all  properties of LGU.
Require the GSO to prepare the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the Audit Team; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant & the GSO to maintain appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards (PPELC), respectively and conduct a periodic reconciliation of records in accordance with Section 114 and 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I
We recommend that the Municipal School Board should strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 100 of RA 7160 and other provisions applicable in the utilization of the fund.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Revenue Collection Clerks, through the Municipal Treasurer, to turn over daily their collections to avoid misuse/misappropriation.  Also, direct the treasurer to verify the Statement of Collections and Accountable Forms to ensure that actual collections are reported and remitted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts, financial statements and reports and accompanying schedules within the period prescribed by existing rules and regulations.
Strictly comply with the information and publicity requirements under COA Circular No. 2013-004 particularly on the design and format of the tarpaulin posted at the project location.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Journal Entry Vouchers in compliance with item (c) of A-23 JEV, LGU-NGAS Instruction Volume.
observations.managementAction: For CY2016, Submitted Vouchers were supported with Journal Entry Vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall ensure that the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations shall be strictly observed.	
Further, immediately discontinue the practice of outright resort to shopping despite absence of conditions that may warrant the use of the said alternative mode.
The Team is not precluded from issuing Notices of Suspension/Disallowance in the event that flagrant violations of the law and the rules are observed from the transactions submitted.

We recommend that Local Chief Executive require the concerned LGU Officials to submit the necessary documentary requirements relative to the Fund Transfers to Barangay in compliance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to institute controls in processing of similar claims. Henceforth, refrain from granting additional Fund Transfers to Barangays with previously unliquidated Fund Transfers.
The Team is not precluded from issuing Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in the event that flagrant violations of the law and the rules are observed from the transactions submitted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall establish a GAD Focal Point System in order to efficiently come up with a GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance with the provisions in the afore-cited Joint Circular.
observations.reason: Management is not aware of the submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report to COA.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the use of the imprest fund system in maintaining the LGU’s Petty Cash Fund and ensure that the same is liquidated at year end in consonance with the provisions of Section 48, NGAS Manual for LGU, Vol. I.
observations.reason: Pertinent documentation requirement partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned LGU Officials to submit the documentary requirements for the Fund Transfers to Barangay in compliance with Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Henceforth, ensure that subsequent Fund Transfers are supported with complete documentation.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere with the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 9184 and Section 9.2.5 COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 relative to the agency’s procurements
observations.reason: Not aware of the additional documentary requirements

observations.recommendation: Instruct all accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to the same accountable officers in compliance with Section 89 of P.D. 1445
observations.managementAction: Instruct all accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to the same accountable officers in compliance with Section 89 of P.D. 1445

observations.recommendation:
Direct all accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate all cash advances.  

Stop the practice of granting subsequent cash advance if previous cash advance have not yet been liquidated.

Adhere strictly to the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management still continues to implement the said recommendations, and parts of unliquidated cash advances have been settled during CY 2013.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to attach all required documents to support the validity of the repairs and maintenance of the municipality’s vehicles consistent with the requirements of Section 362 of COA Circular 91-368.
observations.managementAction: Management still continues to implement the said recommendations
observations.reason: Required pre inspection and evaluation reports were partially accomplished

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should regularly prepare and submit bank reconciliation for its cash in bank account and adjust the books accordingly, pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction:
Management still continues to implement the said recommendations. 

Municipal Accountant did not regularly prepare and submits bank reconciliation statement.
observations.reason: Management has difficulty in reconciling the prior year’s balances.  Bank Reconciliation Statement is still not prepared by the Accountant.

observations.recommendation: The municipality should adhere to the mandates of R.A. 658 for it to avail of financial considerations inherent in insurances should any calamity occur.
observations.managementAction: Only the recently purchased motor vehicles were insured since this was a requirement for the registration to LTO. The Municipality is planning to contract with GSIS for the appraisal of all its properties.
observations.reason: The amount of Property Plant and equipment in the books could not be ascertained because of lack of the necessary supporting ledgers, stock cards, and several vouchers supporting the acquisition of such could not anymore be retrieved as these were either destroyed or perished.
